disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The shutdown came on over about 48 hours, immediately after the patient returned from a demanding multi-day work conference where they had to be constantly social.
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and what was happening just before?
Has anything been going on in their life lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started over the last couple of days, right after they got back from a big multi-day work conference. They were fine before they left, exhausted when they got home, and then just shut down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient cannot say any words, but clearly understands what is said, nods yes/no appropriately, and can send short text replies.
trigger_question_examples: Can they understand you when you talk to them?
Can they nod yes or no, or reply by text?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They understand me completely — they nod yes and no to my questions and they've texted me short replies. They just can't get any words out at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is extremely sensitive to light and sound right now, and keeps holding their head as if it aches.
trigger_question_examples: Are they bothered by light or noise?
Do they seem to have a headache?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — any light or noise makes them flinch and cover up, and they keep pressing on their head like it's hurting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Nobody has noticed the patient feeling hot or feverish.
trigger_question_examples: Have they felt hot or had a temperature?
Does their forehead feel hot to you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, they don't feel hot to me — I don't think they've had a fever.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient can move their neck freely, has no rash, and there is no drooping of the face or weakness down one side — they move both arms and both legs the same.
trigger_question_examples: Can they move their neck / touch their chin to their chest?
Is one side of their face drooping, or is one arm or leg weaker than the other?
Do they have any new rash?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Their neck moves fine, there's no rash, and no — their face isn't drooping and they can move both arms and both legs evenly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient has not eaten or had any fluids for about 12 hours and cannot organise the steps to drink without being talked through it.
trigger_question_examples: Have they been able to eat or drink anything?
Can they get themselves a drink of water?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Nothing for about 12 hours. I have to literally talk them through each step to get them to take a sip — pick up the bottle, open it, drink — or they just don't.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is fully awake and aware — they are not drowsy and have not been difficult to rouse.
trigger_question_examples: Are they alert, or are they drowsy or hard to wake?
Do they respond when you come into the room?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're wide awake — eyes open, they know I'm there and respond to me. They're not drowsy or hard to wake at all, they just won't speak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: This kind of shutdown has happened in a milder form after very busy, very social periods before, and has always eased with rest — but never this severe or this long.
trigger_question_examples: Has anything like this happened to them before?
Do they usually get very drained after intense social periods?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They've had smaller versions of this after really full-on social stretches, and it's always passed with a few days of quiet. But nothing this bad, and never two whole days without speaking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The roommate has not seen or heard the patient express any wish to harm themselves; they seem shut down and exhausted rather than expressing thoughts of self-harm — though the patient cannot currently speak to be asked directly.
trigger_question_examples: Has there been any sign they might want to hurt themselves or that they don't want to be here?
Are you worried at all about their safety?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I haven't seen anything like that. They just seem completely wiped out and overwhelmed, not like they want to hurt themselves. I can't ask them directly right now because they can't talk, but I'd tell you if I was worried about that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient takes no regular prescription medications and has not taken anything new.
trigger_question_examples: Do they take any regular medications?
Have they taken anything new, or any drugs or alcohol?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're not on any regular medications, and they haven't taken anything new that I know of.
